description_long: This kiosk features a prominent open wooden frame structure with a gabled roof, supported by thick timber beams. The roof is composed of exposed wooden rafters, creating a partially open canopy that allows light to filter through. The front of the kiosk displays the word 'WONDEREST' in large, cut-out letters, which cast a shadow onto the ground below. The base of the kiosk is a solid, rectangular platform, while the sides and rear are enclosed with plain, light-colored panels. There are no visible windows or doors, and the interior is open and accessible from the front. The overall design is rustic and inviting, suitable for parks or outdoor urban spaces.
description: A rustic, open-frame wooden kiosk with a gabled roof, solid base, and prominent 'WONDEREST' signage on the front. The structure is partially enclosed with light panels and features exposed timber beams.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open wooden frame, gabled roof, and large 'WONDEREST' signage with shadow on the ground.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the solid side panel and the depth of the wooden rafters, with no signage or openings visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a fully enclosed plain panel and the back of the gabled roof structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the solid side panel and open roof beams.
attribute_signage_type: Cut-out signage integrated into the front wooden frame, displaying the text 'WONDEREST'.
attribute_roof_design: Gabled roof with exposed wooden rafters and no solid covering, creating an open canopy.
attribute_architectural_style: Rustic with minimalist influences, featuring exposed timber and simple geometric forms.
